title: Metathelypteris gracilescens
taxonomy: Metathelypteris gracilescens is classified as a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Metathelypteris and is further classified within the family of Thelypteridaceae. This classification places it within the larger group of vascular plants, specifically the division Pteridophyta. At the rank of kingdom, it is classified as Plantae. Further classification also includes it being a part of the phylum Tracheophyta.
uses: Metathelypteris gracilescens is reported to have various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. It is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties. In some cultures, it is used as a treatment for digestive problems and as an antidote for certain types of poisoning. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Metathelypteris gracilescens for medicinal purposes.
constituents: Unfortunately, I don't have information on the active constituents of Metathelypteris gracilescens.
